input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Check if .NET is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (preferred)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8

# Or for .NET 9:
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to discover the exact name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes android, ios, maccatalyst, tizen sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify it installed
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows-target MAUI apps need the Windows SDK and WinUI/UWP build tools.

```powershell
# Install VS Build Tools with required components
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools

# Then add the required workloads via the VS Installer CLI:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktopBuildTools `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --quiet --wait
```

> **Or** if you're using full Visual Studio, install the **.NET Multi-platform App UI development** workload from the VS Installer GUI.

## 4. Install Android SDK (for Android target)

```powershell
# Option A: Let MAUI auto-provision (easiest)
# Creating/building a MAUI project auto-downloads Android SDK components.
# Just build and accept licenses:
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp
dotnet build -f net8.0-android   # or net9.0-android

# Option B: Manual install via Android CLI tools
# Download command-line tools:
$androidHome = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidHome, "User")

# Download cmdline-tools (check https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only)
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-win-11076708_latest.zip" `
  -OutFile "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip"
Expand-Archive "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip" -DestinationPath "$androidHome\cmdline-tools"

# Fix the expected directory layout
Rename-Item "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\cmdline-tools" "latest"

$sdkmanager = "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# Discover available platforms and build-tools
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-" | Select-Object -Last 5
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "build-tools;"     | Select-Object -Last 5

# Install what MAUI needs (adjust versions from the list output)
& $sdkmanager --install `
  "platforms;android-34" `
  "build-tools;34.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkmanager --licenses   # type 'y' to each prompt
```

## 5. Set Up an Android Emulator (optional)

```powershell
$avdmanager = "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat"

# List available device definitions
& $avdmanager list device

# Create an AVD
& $avdmanager create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" `
  -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" `
  -d "pixel_7"

# Launch it
& "$androidHome\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

## 6. Enable Hardware Acceleration (Hyper-V)

```powershell
# Run as Administrator — needed for Android emulator performance
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All -NoRestart
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName HypervisorPlatform -NoRestart
# Restart after
Restart-Computer
```

## 7. Verify Everything Works

```powershell
# Create a fresh project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Deploy to Android emulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Install -p:AdbTarget="-e"
```

## 8. Discover Versions Dynamically (cheat sheet)

```powershell
# What .NET SDKs are available to install?
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK

# What MAUI workload versions are available?
dotnet workload search maui

# What TFMs does your SDK support?
dotnet new maui --dry-run | Select-String "net\d"

# What Android SDK packages are installed?
& $sdkmanager --list_installed

# What Windows SDK versions are on the machine?
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include" | Select-Object Name
```

---

**Key tips:**
- Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` throughout if you installed .NET 9.
- The first `dotnet build` for Android can take several minutes (downloads dependencies).
- If builds fail with missing Windows SDK errors, ensure the Windows App SDK VS component is installed (step 3).
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a thorough, practical guide.
